failure_mode: A major hyperscaler (e.g., Microsoft, Amazon, Meta) misses its 20% YoY AI capex growth target in a quarterly report, signaling that scaling constraints or efficiency gains are reducing infrastructure buildout urgency.
leading_indicator: Weekly check of cloud provider capex guidance updates via earnings call transcripts and press releases; monitor for any downward revision or capex growth forecast below 20%.
targeted_assumption: Hyperscaler AI capex grows at least 20% YoY each year through 2027.
comment: Falsifier is clearly verifiable within 12 months but the one-company miss does not reliably break the aggregate growth assumption; the indicator's weekly frequency is optimistic given quarterly earnings cycles.

failure_mode: A top non-Google cloud provider (e.g., Amazon or Microsoft) announces a major inference or training deployment using AMD MI300X or proprietary ASICs, indicating NVDA's accelerator share slips below 75%.
leading_indicator: Weekly monitoring of AMD's press releases, hyperscaler quarterly earnings calls for new accelerator partnerships, and technical benchmarks from MLPerf; sudden adoption announcements or benchmarks favoring alternatives.
targeted_assumption: NVDA accelerator share stays above 75% as competitors (AMD, Intel, in-house silicon) fail to close the software/ecosystem gap.
comment: The falsifier is verifiable within 12 months, directly breaks the share assumption, uses named but slightly qualitative indicators, and the 0.25 probability is defensible.

failure_mode: NVDA reports gross margins falling to 63% or below in a quarterly filing, driven by ASP erosion as supply constraints ease and competition intensifies for non-flagship SKUs.
leading_indicator: Weekly tracking of H100/B200 spot rental prices on cloud GPU marketplaces (e.g., Vast.ai, RunPod) and reseller prices on secondary markets; a sustained 10%+ month-over-month decline in average per-GPU-hour rental rates signals impending ASP pressure.
targeted_assumption: Gross margins remain above 65% as supply constraints ease without triggering ASP compression.
comment: The falsifier is specific and verifiable within 9 months, directly breaks the assumption, has a concrete weekly leading indicator, and the 0.15 probability is defensible for a sharp margin erosion given NVDA's pricing power.

failure_mode: PCAOB inspection access is not renewed or materially restricted, raising delisting risk
leading_indicator: Weekly SEC and PCAOB statements on negotiations, US-China political tensions headlines, and auditor registration updates
targeted_assumption: PCAOB inspection access to China-based auditors is renewed at every annual review through 2027 without material restriction.
comment: Event is verifiable within 6 months via official statements and directly breaks the assumption, but leading indicator lacks a single quantifiable metric and 35% probability seems high for a short-horizon binary event.

failure_mode: Alibaba Cloud revenue growth falls below 15% YoY in any of the upcoming quarterly reports
leading_indicator: Monthly cloud infrastructure spending data from Synergy Research Group or IDC, and Alibaba Cloud's quarterly client wins / capex guidance in earnings calls
targeted_assumption: Alibaba Cloud revenue grows at least 15% YoY in each of the next eight reported quarters.
comment: The leading indicator data is only quarterly, not weekly checkable, and the 0.7 probability massively understates the near-certainty of below-15% growth given Alibaba Cloud's current single-digit growth rates.

failure_mode: China NBS retail sales ex-auto grows ≤5% YoY in at least two of the next four calendar quarters
leading_indicator: Weekly Wind Information consumer spending tracker, major e-commerce platform GMV trends, and monthly NBS advance retail trade estimates
targeted_assumption: Chinese household-consumption growth (NBS retail sales ex-auto) exceeds 5% YoY in at least three of the next four calendar quarters.
comment: The falsifier is directly verifiable via quarterly NBS releases within 12 months, logically breaks the assumption, uses concrete leading indicators (weekly Wind tracker, monthly NBS estimates) with minor vagueness on e-commerce GMV, and a 0.4 probability is defensible given macro uncertainty.

failure_mode: Political gridlock over debt ceiling leads to a continuing resolution flat-lining FY2026 DoD budget, preventing 3% nominal growth.

targeted_assumption: US Department of Defense base topline (excluding OCO) grows at least 3% nominal each year through FY2028.

failure_mode: Economic recession in Germany and France causes defence budget cuts, resulting in fewer than 18 NATO members meeting 2% by end of 2027.
leading_indicator: Weekly polling of European Commission economic sentiment indicators and national budget draft submissions
targeted_assumption: At least 18 of 32 NATO members meet or exceed the 2% of GDP defence spending floor by end of 2027.
comment: The event is not verifiable within 12 months (2027 outcome), the recession cuts may not break the 18-country assumption, weekly data sources are vague/unavailable, and 0.45 seems overstated for a 12-month chain.

failure_mode: Major F-35 program restructuring or foreign order cancellations cause Lockheed backlog to slip below $150B at FY2025 year-end.

targeted_assumption: Lockheed Martin's reported backlog stays above $150B at every fiscal year-end through 2027.
